---
pagetitle: "StudyStats — Service menu & rates"
description: "A rough guide to per-deliverable pricing for statistical consulting. Every project is individually scoped, priced and invoiced."
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    toc: false
css: styles.css
---

```{=html}
<style>
  .ss-rates{max-width:1080px;margin:0 auto;padding:3rem 1.25rem 1rem}
  .ss-rates h1{font-size:clamp(1.9rem,3.4vw,2.6rem);margin:0 0 .5rem;text-align:center;letter-spacing:-.5px}
  .ss-rates .lead{text-align:center;color:#b8b8b8;max-width:760px;margin:0 auto 1.25rem;font-size:1.05rem;line-height:1.55}
  .ss-rates .currency-note{text-align:center;color:#7a7a7a;font-size:.82rem;margin:0 0 2.25rem}
  .ss-rates h2.ss-cat{font-size:1.25rem;margin:2.25rem 0 .35rem;letter-spacing:-.3px;color:#fff}
  .ss-rates .cat-blurb{color:#9a9a9a;margin:0 0 .9rem;font-size:.92rem}
  table.ss-pricetable{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;background:#1b1b1b;border:1px solid #2a2a2a;border-radius:10px;overflow:hidden;margin-bottom:.5rem}
  table.ss-pricetable th,table.ss-pricetable td{padding:.78rem 1rem;text-align:left;border-bottom:1px solid #2a2a2a;vertical-align:top}
  table.ss-pricetable th{background:#222;color:#cfcfcf;font-size:.74rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.5px;font-weight:600}
  table.ss-pricetable tr:last-child td{border-bottom:none}
  table.ss-pricetable td.label{font-weight:600;color:#fff;width:30%;white-space:nowrap}
  table.ss-pricetable td.desc{color:#bdbdbd;font-size:.91rem;line-height:1.5}
  table.ss-pricetable td.price{text-align:right;color:#fff;font-weight:600;white-space:nowrap;width:14%;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  .ss-callout{background:linear-gradient(135deg,#1b1b1b,#2a2a2a);border-radius:12px;padding:1.6rem 1.5rem;margin:2rem 0;border:1px solid #2a2a2a}
  .ss-callout h3{margin:0 0 .5rem;font-size:1.05rem}
  .ss-callout p{color:#cfcfcf;margin:0;line-height:1.55;font-size:.94rem}
  .ss-disclaimer{background:#1b1b1b;border:1px solid #4a4a4a;border-left:3px solid #fff;border-radius:8px;padding:1rem 1.25rem;margin:2.25rem 0 0;color:#cfcfcf;font-size:.92rem;line-height:1.55}
  .ss-disclaimer strong{color:#fff}
  .ss-faq{margin:2.5rem 0 4rem}
  .ss-faq details{background:#1b1b1b;border:1px solid #2a2a2a;border-radius:8px;padding:.95rem 1.25rem;margin-bottom:.55rem}
  .ss-faq summary{cursor:pointer;font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.96rem;list-style:none}
  .ss-faq summary::-webkit-details-marker{display:none}
  .ss-faq summary::before{content:"+";display:inline-block;width:1.1rem;color:#9a9a9a;font-weight:700}
  .ss-faq details[open] summary::before{content:"–"}
  .ss-faq details[open] summary{margin-bottom:.55rem}
  .ss-faq details p{color:#bdbdbd;margin:0;line-height:1.55;font-size:.92rem;padding-left:1.1rem}
  .ss-cta-row{display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;gap:.75rem;justify-content:center;margin-top:1rem}
  .ss-btn{display:inline-block;padding:.85rem 1.6rem;border-radius:6px;font-weight:600;font-size:1rem;text-decoration:none!important;border:1.5px solid transparent;transition:transform .08s,box-shadow .15s,background .15s}
  .ss-btn-primary{background:#fff;color:#121212!important}
  .ss-btn-primary:hover{transform:translateY(-1px);box-shadow:0 6px 18px rgba(255,255,255,.12)}
  .ss-btn-secondary{background:transparent;color:#fff!important;border-color:#fff}
  .ss-btn-secondary:hover{background:rgba(255,255,255,.08)}
  .ss-final{background:linear-gradient(135deg,#1b1b1b,#2a2a2a);border:1px solid #2a2a2a;border-radius:14px;padding:2.5rem 1.5rem;text-align:center;margin:3rem auto 5rem;max-width:900px}
  .ss-final h2{margin:0 0 .5rem}
  .ss-final p{color:#cfcfcf;max-width:600px;margin:0 auto 1.25rem}
</style>
```

::: {.ss-rates}

# Service menu & rough guide pricing

::: {.lead}
Below is the StudyStats service menu — a transparent reference for the kinds of deliverables we produce and what each typically costs. Use it to estimate your project. Every engagement is then **individually scoped, priced and invoiced** based on your specific data, objectives and timeline.
:::

<p class="currency-note">All prices in <strong>ZAR</strong>, exclusive of VAT. Effective May 2026. Indicative only — see disclaimer below.</p>

<h2 class="ss-cat">Descriptive analysis &amp; comparison</h2>
<p class="cat-blurb">Tables, graphs and basic comparisons that describe your sample.</p>

<table class="ss-pricetable">
<thead><tr><th>Item</th><th>Description</th><th style="text-align:right;">Price</th></tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr><td class="label">Simple descriptive table</td><td class="desc">Counts, percentages, means, medians. One table = descriptive statistics from the same denominator.</td><td class="price">R300</td></tr>
<tr><td class="label">Simple graph</td><td class="desc">Bar charts, histograms, x–y scatter plots.</td><td class="price">R300</td></tr>
<tr><td class="label">Advanced descriptive table</td><td class="desc">Stratified by groups, summary statistics across multiple variables.</td><td class="price">R500</td></tr>
<tr><td class="label">Advanced visualisation</td><td class="desc">Boxplots, violin plots, multi-panel figures, coefficient (forest) plots.</td><td class="price">R600</td></tr>
<tr><td class="label">Data wrangling for descriptives</td><td class="desc">Cleaning, merging or reshaping data (per dataset).</td><td class="price">R400</td></tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<h2 class="ss-cat">Simple modelling <span style="color:#9a9a9a;font-weight:400;font-size:.85rem;">— correlations, odds ratios, risk ratios</span></h2>
<p class="cat-blurb">Univariate models for unadjusted associations and effect sizes.</p>

<table class="ss-pricetable">
<thead><tr><th>Item</th><th>Description</th><th style="text-align:right;">Price</th></tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr><td class="label">Univariate regression model</td><td class="desc">Linear, logistic, Poisson, Cox, etc.</td><td class="price">R600</td></tr>
<tr><td class="label">Model diagnostics</td><td class="desc">Residual plots, fit statistics, VIF, assumptions check.</td><td class="price">R400</td></tr>
<tr><td class="label">Data wrangling for model</td><td class="desc">Preparing the analysis dataset.</td><td class="price">R400</td></tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<h2 class="ss-cat">Modelling adjusted for confounders</h2>
<p class="cat-blurb">Multivariable models with sensitivity analyses and full diagnostics.</p>

<table class="ss-pricetable">
<thead><tr><th>Item</th><th>Description</th><th style="text-align:right;">Price</th></tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr><td class="label">Multivariable regression model</td><td class="desc">Linear, logistic, Poisson, Cox, etc.</td><td class="price">R900</td></tr>
<tr><td class="label">Model diagnostics</td><td class="desc">Residual plots, AIC/BIC, ROC, calibration.</td><td class="price">R500</td></tr>
<tr><td class="label">Sensitivity analyses</td><td class="desc">Alternative specifications, sub-group models.</td><td class="price">R500</td></tr>
<tr><td class="label">Data wrangling for model</td><td class="desc">Handling missingness, transformations, variable coding.</td><td class="price">R500</td></tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<h2 class="ss-cat">Writing &amp; interpretation</h2>
<p class="cat-blurb">Methodology, results and interpretation text — drop-in for your manuscript or thesis.</p>

<table class="ss-pricetable">
<thead><tr><th>Item</th><th>Description</th><th style="text-align:right;">Price</th></tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr><td class="label">Methodology section</td><td class="desc">Clear description of methods used; adequate for short manuscript (100–200 words).</td><td class="price">R600</td></tr>
<tr><td class="label">Interpretation of results</td><td class="desc">Short narrative summary of findings — a starting point for the discussion, completed by the PI.</td><td class="price">R600</td></tr>
<tr><td class="label">Formal results — descriptives</td><td class="desc">Formal results writing of descriptive statistics (&lt; 100 words).</td><td class="price">R500</td></tr>
<tr><td class="label">Formal results — simple modelling</td><td class="desc">Formal results writing of simple modelling statistics (&lt; 100 words).</td><td class="price">R500</td></tr>
<tr><td class="label">Formal results — complex modelling</td><td class="desc">Formal results writing of complex modelling (&lt; 100 words).</td><td class="price">R500</td></tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<h2 class="ss-cat">Diagnostic performance studies</h2>
<p class="cat-blurb">For sensitivity / specificity, ROC and prediction-model accuracy work.</p>

<table class="ss-pricetable">
<thead><tr><th>Item</th><th>Description</th><th style="text-align:right;">Price</th></tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr><td class="label">Sensitivity &amp; specificity</td><td class="desc">Basic 2×2 table (test vs. gold standard) with confidence intervals.</td><td class="price">R500</td></tr>
<tr><td class="label">Predictive values (PPV, NPV)</td><td class="desc">Added calculation, with prevalence adjustment if needed.</td><td class="price">R400</td></tr>
<tr><td class="label">ROC curve (single test/model)</td><td class="desc">ROC curve and AUC with confidence intervals.</td><td class="price">R700</td></tr>
<tr><td class="label">ROC curve comparison</td><td class="desc">Compare AUCs of two or more tests / models.</td><td class="price">R900</td></tr>
<tr><td class="label">Calibration / agreement</td><td class="desc">Hosmer–Lemeshow, Brier score, kappa.</td><td class="price">R600</td></tr>
<tr><td class="label">Decision curve analysis</td><td class="desc">Net benefit curves (optional advanced).</td><td class="price">R900</td></tr>
<tr><td class="label">Data wrangling for accuracy</td><td class="desc">Preparing dataset, binary outcomes, thresholds.</td><td class="price">R500</td></tr>
<tr><td class="label">Methodology write-up</td><td class="desc">Clear explanation of diagnostic methods used.</td><td class="price">R600</td></tr>
<tr><td class="label">Interpretation write-up</td><td class="desc">Plain-language summary of accuracy results.</td><td class="price">R600</td></tr>
<tr><td class="label">Formal results section</td><td class="desc">Journal-ready tables and figures.</td><td class="price">R800</td></tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<div class="ss-disclaimer">
<strong>This menu is a rough guide.</strong> Every project is individually scoped and quoted based on the complexity of your data, the number of objectives and your timeline. Some items combine, some need extending, and some don't apply. After our free 15-minute call you'll receive an itemised written quote drawn from this menu — the prices on your quote are what you pay, full stop.
</div>

<div class="ss-callout">
<h3>Revisions, updates &amp; ad-hoc work</h3>
<p>The first round of revisions on each deliverable is included free (within 14 days of delivery). Subsequent revisions, supervisor changes and reviewer responses are billed in <strong>30-minute blocks at R400</strong>, tracked transparently and invoiced monthly.</p>
</div>

<div class="ss-callout">
<h3>Payment terms</h3>
<p><strong>50% deposit</strong> secures your project start; the balance is due on delivery. Quotes are valid for 30 days. EFT and PayFast accepted. Tax invoice issued on payment. StudyStats is a registered vendor with several South African universities — purchase-order-compliant invoicing available on request.</p>
</div>

## Common questions

::: {.ss-faq}

<details><summary>Why per-deliverable instead of hourly?</summary><p>Per-deliverable pricing maps directly to what you and your supervisor already think in — "Table 1, Table 2, regression model, methods section". You see exactly what you're paying for and there are no hourly surprises if a model takes longer to run.</p></details>

<details><summary>What if my data needs more cleaning than expected?</summary><p>The cleaning fee is set during the scoping call once we've seen the data. If reality differs from what we agreed, we re-quote <em>before</em> doing the extra work — never after.</p></details>

<details><summary>Can I buy just one or two items?</summary><p>Yes. Many clients only need a sample size calculation, an ethics statistics section, or a single table. Minimum invoice R500.</p></details>

<details><summary>Do you charge for the discovery call?</summary><p>No. The first 15-minute call is free. If you need a deeper working session before committing, that can be booked as a paid scoping consult — and the fee is credited to your project if you proceed within 30 days.</p></details>

<details><summary>Can my institution pay directly?</summary><p>Yes. We can issue purchase-order-compliant invoices and tax invoices on request, and are already onboarded with several South African universities.</p></details>

:::

:::

::: {.ss-final}
## Ready to scope your project?
Bring your research question and your deadline — we'll send back an itemised quote drawn from this menu, tailored to what you actually need.

::: {.ss-cta-row}
<a href="contact.qmd" class="ss-btn ss-btn-primary">Book a free 15-min consult →</a>
<a href="how_it_works.qmd" class="ss-btn ss-btn-secondary">See how it works</a>
:::
:::
